item.title: NASA-Funded Study Shows How Controlled Burns Help Sequoias Survive Wildfires - NASA Science
item.description: A new study, funded by NASA, found that giant sequoias were nearly four times more likely to survive extreme wildfires in forests that have been managed with prescribed burns, which decrease tree mortality.

item.title: Amendment 64: New Opportunity: A.15 Biosphere - NASA Science
item.description: NASA's Biosphere research area focuses on research that provides foundational knowledge on interactions between and within terrestrial and aquatic ecosystems,

item.title: NASA, GE Aerospace Work Enables Hybrid-Electric Flight Demonstration
item.description: NASA, GE Aerospace Work Enables Hybrid-Electric Flight Demonstration An aircraft powered by a megawatt-class hybrid-electric engine developed in collaboration with NASA and built by GE Aerospace, demonstrated flight of an innovation that can inform new generations of fuel-saving aircraft power systems.

item.title: XR SIG Seminar, 7 August 2026 - NASA Science
item.description: A TDAMM Mission Concept Offered by the X-ray Timing Science Analysis Group: the Dynamic Extreme Universe in X-rays (DEUS-X) Machine

item.title: New NASA Earth Missions Gear Up to Start Science Flights - NASA Science
item.description: The first of six new airborne campaigns will hit the skies this summer.

item.title: XR SIG Seminar, 24 July 2026 - NASA Science
item.description: Hybrid CMOS Detectors. Speaker: Abe Falcone, Penn State

item.title: Amendment 63: New Opportunity: A.14 Atmosphere - NASA Science
item.description: The Atmosphere research program is soliciting proposals for data analysis and modeling of NASA supported airborne campaign data. NASA’s Earth Science Division

item.title: From Hampton to Mars: How NASA Langley Helped Land on the Red Planet - NASA
item.description: Fifty years ago, NASA’s Viking 1 and 2 landers made the first successful landings on Mars, opening a new era in planetary exploration. Behind that achievement

item.title: NASA Sets Briefings for SpaceX Crew-13 Mission to Space Station - NASA
item.description: Mission leadership will provide an overview of NASA’s SpaceX Crew‑13 mission at 12 p.m. EDT. Next, crew members will discuss their training and mission

item.title: Expedition 74 Turns Attention to Departing Crewmates - NASA
item.description: Three Expedition 74 crew members are packing up cargo and handing over their responsibilities with less than one week to go before returning to Earth. The International Space Station’s three newest crewmates are already in the second week of their mission getting used to life on orbit and researching how microgravity affects the human body.
